skill.name: core-components
skill.description: Design system guidance for building UI with core components, design tokens, layout patterns, and component usage rules.
skill.summary: Build consistent interfaces with core UI components, design tokens, and reusable layout patterns.
skill.author: ChrisWiles

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown fences, TSX examples, token names, and prose. No executable scripts, network behavior, credential access, prompt injection, or cryptographic implementation were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings map to Markdown code fences, inline token examples, and TSX component snippets. These examples document UI usage and do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are Markdown or TSX documentation examples, not Ruby or shell execution. No executable skill script or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings map to words such as description, Design System, Design Tokens, and design tokens. No hash, cipher, signature, or encryption logic appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are UI design prose and token guidance. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic code or insecure algorithm use.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when screens use raw components and hard-coded styles. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use shared components, tokens, and layout patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Box, HStack, VStack, Text, Button, Input, and Card components.
Provides spacing, color, and typography token examples for consistent styling.
Shows screen, form, and list item layout patterns using the core component library.
Identifies anti-patterns such as raw platform components, inline styles, and hard-coded values.
Guides component prop design with token-based options and reusable variants.
limitations: Does not include implementation files for the referenced component library.
Assumes the project already has compatible core components and tokens.
Does not define accessibility rules beyond the shown component usage patterns.
Does not provide automated linting or runtime enforcement.

use_cases.title: Standardize App Screens
use_cases.description: Create screens that use shared layout components, spacing tokens, and semantic color tokens.

use_cases.title: Review UI Consistency
use_cases.description: Find raw platform components, inline styles, and hard-coded values before code review.

use_cases.title: Document Component Usage
use_cases.description: Explain expected component variants, token choices, and layout patterns for product teams.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Raw UI to Core Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen and describe how to replace raw platform components with core components and design tokens.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Token-Based Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a form layout using VStack, Input, Button, spacing tokens, and validation states from the core component patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Component for Design System Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this component for hard-coded values, raw platform components, inline styles, and missing token-based props.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Reusable Component API
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable component API that uses token-based props, variants, and core layout primitives.

output_examples.input: A screen uses raw views, inline spacing, and hard-coded colors.
output_examples.output: The response recommends core layout components, spacing tokens, semantic color tokens, and a clearer screen structure.

output_examples.input: A product team needs a reusable card pattern.
output_examples.output: The response describes token-based padding, variant choices, header and body structure, and consistent text styling.

output_examples.input: A pull request mixes platform text components with design system text components.
output_examples.output: The response flags the inconsistency and recommends the shared Text component with typography and color tokens.

best_practices: Use semantic design tokens for spacing, color, typography, and border values.
Prefer core components before raw platform components in application UI.
Define reusable component props with constrained token values and documented variants.
anti_patterns: Using raw platform components when a matching core component exists.
Hard-coding pixel values, hex colors, or font weights in component markup.
Adding inline styles instead of token-based props or shared variants.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build interfaces that follow a shared core component library and design token system.

faq.question: Does it include component source code?
faq.answer: No. It provides usage guidance and patterns for an existing component library.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it review existing UI code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide reviews for raw components, inline styles, and hard-coded design values.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to React Native?
faq.answer: The examples use React Native style components, but the design token guidance is broadly useful.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and does not define network operations.
